comments.comment: I'll keep this list live and updated. Whenever we finalize an item—like layout, naming, or post-purchase flow—I'll remove it from this canvas automatically so you're always working off a current to-do list.

Flagged for consistency check: ensure that this canvas reflects the finalized structure, tone, and logic established in the latest rebrand documents. Remove or revise any outdated structures, naming conventions, or tone inconsistencies.
Double-check that product presentation here reflects updated tone rules—English headlines, Hebrew CTAs, and direct grid-based pricing logic.
Ensure this structure aligns with RH/Habitat tone restraint and that copy tone matches the finalized premium Israeli voice—concise, non-verbose, no over-explanation.
Review top navigation structure and hero logic for possible conflicts with the finalized product page format and Brand Steward tone enforcement.
This appears fully aligned. Double-confirm that all tone, structure, and language logic matches the latest version—including 'Where This Bouquet Lives' and 'Customer Words'.
Verify that the hybrid model—hidden purchasable leftovers and sacred lookbook presentation—is clearly described with no contradictions.
Confirm that none of the aesthetic or tone values here conflict with RH fallback logic or Efrat’s local tone rules.
Check that this safeguard correctly governs all design-first canvases and won’t allow future conflicts with tone or UX standards.
Verified: all sections align with the latest brand direction. No inconsistencies found in tone, layout logic, or naming structure.
Confirmed: all elements match the finalized homepage strategy—minimal nav, seasonal-first layout, English/Hebrew tone balance, and RH-aligned UX.
Aligned with latest brand structure. No inconsistencies found. This grid layout follows RH visual minimalism, Efrat's tone rules, and Abloh's visual symbolism. No revision needed.
Confirmed alignment: tone, structure, and copy format fully reflect current standards—clean, restrained, and RH-compliant. No conflicting legacy content.
No inconsistencies. Homepage layout reflects final menu logic, copy tone, and grid structure. Fully compliant with RH restraint and Israeli cultural clarity.
Fully aligned with updated brand vision. Product structure, copy tone, visual order, and CTA format are consistent with final hierarchy. No revision required.
Archive structure reflects approved non-commercial logic and Abloh-inspired sacredness. Visual style and UX framing are consistent with current brand system. No updates needed.
Creative hierarchy is consistent with the Abloh model you approved—no contradictions with RH, Habitat, or Efrat. Clean integration across all layers. Verified.
Safeguard logic is properly tied to RH baseline. Serves as override for any visual drift. Fully aligned—no conflict with existing structure.
